mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, driving corporate revenue expectations higher and yielding positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns, with a logistic regression coefficient insignificant at p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect strengthening manufacturing capacity utilization, which directly boosts corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases correlate with negative 20-day SPX returns during expansion phases, or out-of-sample predictive accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls indicate robust labor demand, which supports consumer spending trajectories and produces positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 5-day forward SPX direction across rolling 12-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation readings exceeding 0.4% force central banks to accelerate rate hikes, compressing equity valuations through higher discount rates and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in > 60% of months where CPIAUCSL rises > 0.4% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly real personal consumption expenditures confirms broad-based economic momentum, reducing recession probabilities and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Combined GDPC1 and RSAFS growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns, with a joint model AUC below 0.55 over walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the industrial production index during late-cycle expansions signal manufacturing contraction and impending earnings downgrades, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases correlate with positive 5-day SPX returns in > 50% of observed cases.
